mainEntity.name: How much money can I get for lost wages if my RSI keeps me out of work in Idaho?

acceptedAnswer.text: You generally receive 66.6% of your average weekly wage in Idaho while you are unable to work. These benefits continue until you reach maximum medical improvement or your employer provides a job within your physical restrictions.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Idaho, your "lost wage" benefits (officially called Temporary Total Disability or TTD) are generally 2/3 of your average weekly wage, subject to state minimums and maximums. These payments continue as long as you are in what's called the "healing period"—meaning a doctor says you cannot work or your employer cannot accommodate your RSI-related restrictions.

The amount is based on your earnings from the 52 weeks prior to the injury.

Key Factors:
- Your documented "Average Weekly Wage" (AWW).
- The current Idaho state cap on benefits.
- Whether you are totally off work or just working fewer hours (TPD).

If the insurer disagrees with your doctor about your ability to work, they may stop these payments. 

Having a lawyer ensures your wage calculation is correct—including overtime and bonuses—and that your payments don't stop prematurely.
